page: Amazon Web Services i8ge.metal-48xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server powered by 192 dedicated physical cores of the ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.7 GHz. The system features 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It is equipped with a massive local storage capacity of 120,000 GB on NVMe SSDs, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, scoring exceptionally high in multi-core stress-ng tests and Geekbench multi-core metrics. Its memory subsystem achieves top-tier read and write bandwidth, which translates to superior performance in memory-intensive operations and database tasks. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark database operations also rank in the top tier. However, single-core performance, including single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core scores, remains in the average tier, and LLM text generation speeds are poor, highlighting a tradeoff where parallelized workloads are heavily favored over single-threaded execution.

Given its bare-metal architecture, high core count, and massive 120 TB local NVMe storage, this server offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for large-scale, I/O-intensive deployments. It is highly suited for high-throughput database operations, distributed data stores, large-scale file systems, and high-performance computing tasks that require massive parallel processing and rapid local storage access.
description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.metal-48xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server featuring 192 dedicated physical cores on the 64-bit ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 architecture clocked at 2.7 GHz. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory and a massive 120,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and superior database operation throughput, though single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are comparatively average to poor. This resource-dense profile offers excellent qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized, storage-heavy workloads. It is best suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput data processing, and distributed storage systems.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.metal-48xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server featuring 192 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 120 TB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 Graviton cores, 1536 GB RAM, and 120 TB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A bare-metal, storage-optimized ARM platform featuring one hundred and ninety-two dedicated cores and one hundred and twenty terabytes of local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.7 GHz
120,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for massive data capacity
300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfers
Top-tier multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Optimized for large-scale databases, distributed storage, and parallel data processing
